zh.Bodorgan, Isle of Anglesey, Wales: 英國威爾斯安格萊波多爾根
zh.lifeSummary: 當Angharad ferch Gwilym or William於1457年出生在英國威爾斯安格萊波多爾根時，她的父親Gwilym ap Evan22歲，母親Gwenhwyfar ferch Llywelyn30歲。她於1478年與Llewellyn ap Heylin在威爾士結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世在英國威爾斯安格萊。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Angharad ferch Gwilym or William于1457年出生在英国威尔斯安格萊波多爾根时，她的父亲Gwilym ap Evan22岁，母亲Gwenhwyfar ferch Llywelyn30岁。她于1478年与Llewellyn ap Heylin在威尔士结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世在英国威尔斯安格萊。

nameMeanings.name: or
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Jewish (Israeli): from Hebrew or ‘light’, a Hebraicized form of various original Ashkenazic surnames.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 柯, see   Ke   1.
Chinese: Hokkien form of the surname 胡, see   Hu   1.

variants.name: Ke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Heng, Yong, Bin, Song, Yan, Zhi, Cheng, Chih-Ming, Chun-Yen, Fan, Fong, Gang.  Vietnamese Lan, Hai, Hoa, Le Van, Mui, Tuy.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 柯, meaning ‘bough, stalk’ in Chinese: (i) from the first element of the personal name Ke Lu (柯盧), personal name of a prince of the state of Wu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the first element of the placename Ke Ze (柯澤), the name of a fief (located in Shandong province) granted to an official in the state of Qi during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) traced back to the Qiang ethnic group in ancient western China and the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China. (iv) adopted as a surname by the Ke Ba (柯拔) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).
Chinese: variant of   Ge  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name William, Willam, Willem, an Old Picard and Norman French form of ancient Germanic Willihelm, composed of the elements wil- ‘will, desire’ + helm- ‘helmet, protection’. This Norman personal name became widely used in England, Scotland and Wales after the Conquest, mainly no doubt in honor of the Conqueror himself. By the 13th century it had already become the most popular personal name in England. A Parisian form of the name Guillaume was also sometimes used (see Gillam ), which in medieval Wales was adopted as Gwilym (see Gwilliam ).
Americanized form (translation into English) of French   Guillaume  .

variants.name: Memoli
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Ciro, Francesco, Luciano, Natale, Nino.
Italian:
patronymic or plural form of a pet form of thepersonal name Guglielmo, Italian form of William .

variants.name: McElligott
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brigid, Brendan, Seamus.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Uileagóid, a patronymic from a pet form of the Gaelic form of the personal name William .

variants.name: McWilliam
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scottish Iain, Morag.

Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Uilleim, a patronymic from a Gaelicized form of William , also Anglicized as Williamson in Scotland. The name is thought to be mainly Scottish in Ulster, though there has been some confusion with McQuillan in County Down. In Connacht, McWilliam was a branch of Burke .

variants.name: Wilcock
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): from the Middle English personal name Wilcoc, a pet form of William (composed of the elements Will 1 + the hypocoristic suffix -cok; see Cocke ).

variants.name: McWilliams
meanings: Irish (northern): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Uilleim, a patronymic from a Gaelicized form of William , with excrescent English -s. In Ireland it sometimes represents a partial Gaelicization of Welsh Williams .

variants.name: Lemmo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Donato, Gino.
Italian (southern): from the personal name Lemmo, a pet form of Guglielmo, an equivalent of William . Compare Lembo .

variants.name: Wilk
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Mariusz, Stanislaw, Tadeusz, Zygmunt, Andrzej, Bogdan, Boguslaw, Casimir, Krzysztof, Stanislawa, Boleslaw, Darek.

Polish: from the vocabulary word wilk ‘wolf’, probably applied as a nickname for someone thought to resemble a wolf or connected with wolves in some way. Alternatively, from an Old Slavic personal name based on vlъkъ ‘wolf’.
English (Staffordshire): from Middle English Wilke, a pet form of William and probably a shortening of Wilkin . The usage was very likely Flemish in origin.

variants.name: Wilkin
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Wilkin, a pet form of William introduced to England and Scotland by Flemings and Picards after the Norman Conquest.
North German: variant of   Wilken  .

variants.name: Gillam
meanings: English: variant of   Gilliam  .
Welsh: Anglicized form of   Gwilliam  .

variants.name: Lembo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Carmelo, Domenic, Pietro, Alfio, Amelio, Antonio, Biagio, Dante, Giovani, Giovanni.
Italian (southern): from a hypercorrected form of Lemmo, a pet form of Guglièlmo, an equivalent of William .

nameMeanings.name: Angharad
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Old Celtic intensive prefix an- + cār ‘love’ + the noun suffix -ad. This was the name of the mother of the 12th-century chronicler Giraldus Cambrensis (‘Gerald the Welshman’). In the Mabinogi, Angharad Golden Hand at first rejects Peredur's suit, but later falls in love with him when he comes back as the unknown Mute Knight. The name has been strongly revived in Wales since the 1940s, borne, for example, by the actress Angharad Rees ( 1949-2012 ).

nameMeanings.name: ferch
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: variant of   Ferg  . Compare   Verch  .
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förtsch (see Foertsch ).
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förch (see Foerch ).

variants.name: Ferg
meanings: 1  ‘Sharp blade’.
2  ‘Wind’.

historicEvents.description: Charles I of Spain became Emperor Charles V of the Holy Roman Empire.

en.lifeSummary: When Angharad ferch Gwilym or William was born in 1457, in Bodorgan, Anglesey, Wales, United Kingdom, her father, Gwilym ap Evan, was 22 and her mother, Gwenhwyfar ferch Llywelyn, was 30. She married Llewellyn ap Heylin in 1478, in Wales. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 1 daughter. She died in Anglesey, Wales, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Angharad ferch Gwilym or William est née en 1457, à Bodorgan, Anglesey, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, son père, Gwilym ap Evan, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Gwenhwyfar ferch Llywelyn, avait 30 ans. Elle a épousé Llewellyn ap Heylin en 1478, à Galles. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée à Anglesey, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Angharad ferch Gwilym or William 1457 in Bodorgan, Anglesey, Wales, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Gwilym ap Evan 22 und ihre Mutter Gwenhwyfar ferch Llywelyn 30. Sie hat 1478 in Wales Llewellyn ap Heylin geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb in Anglesey, Wales, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Angharad ferch Gwilym or William è nata il nel 1457 nel Bodorgan, Anglesey, Galles, Regno Unito, sua padre, Gwilym ap Evan, aveva 22 anni e sua madre, Gwenhwyfar ferch Llywelyn, aveva 30 anni. Lei sposato Llewellyn ap Heylin nel 1478, nel Galles. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel Anglesey, Galles, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Angharad ferch Gwilym or Williamが1457年にイギリスウェールズアングレセイボドルガンで生まれた時，父Gwilym ap Evanは22歳，母Gwenhwyfar ferch Llywelynは30歳であった。1478年にウェールズでLlewellyn ap Heylinと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女はイギリスウェールズアングレセイで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Angharad ferch Gwilym or William(이)가 1457년 영국 웨일스 앤글레시 보도르건에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Gwilym ap Evan(은)는 22세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Gwenhwyfar ferch Llywelyn(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 1478년 웨일스에서 Llewellyn ap Heylin(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 웨일스 앤글레시에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Angharad ferch Gwilym or William nasceu em 1457, em Bodorgan, Anglesey, Gales, United Kingdom, seu pai, Gwilym ap Evan, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Gwenhwyfar ferch Llywelyn, tinha 30 anos. Ela casou-se com Llewellyn ap Heylin em 1478, em Gales. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em Anglesey, Gales, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Angharad ferch Gwilym or William родилась в 1457 г. в местности бодорган, англеси, Уэльс, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Gwilym ap Evan было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Gwenhwyfar ferch Llywelyn, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Llewellyn ap Heylin в 1478 г. в местности Уэльс. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она умерла в местности англеси, Уэльс, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Angharad ferch Gwilym or William nació en 1457, en Bodorgan, Isla Oscura, Gales, Reino Unido, su papá, Gwilym ap Evan, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Gwenhwyfar ferch Llywelyn, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Llewellyn ap Heylin en 1478, en Gales. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en Isla Oscura, Gales, Reino Unido.
